name: Delphi Ventures
notes: Crypto and Web3-focused venture capital firm launched in 2020. Thesis-driven, high-conviction fund investing in blockchain, DeFi, and crypto-AI intersection. name: The Operating Group
notes: Small venture capital fund based in Road Town, British Virgin Islands, investing primarily in Seed-stage blockchain and crypto startups. Has invested in 5 companies including Mythos Ventures. Team of 2 partners. Limited public information available.

name: Players Philanthropy Fund, Inc.
notes: Nonprofit fiscal sponsor (501(c)(3)) providing tax-exempt status, back-office services, and operational support to 500+ organizations and family funds. Founded in 2010 by retired NFL player Matt Stover and nonprofit professional Seth McDonnell. Widely used within the effective altruism community as

name: Survival & Flourishing Fund
notes: Major EA/longtermist grantmaker focused on long-term survival and flourishing of sentient life. Founded 2019, primarily funded by Jaan Tallinn (Skype co-founder). Distributed ~$152M since inception, $34.9M in 2025 alone — 86% to AI safety. Second-largest AI safety funder after Open Philanthropy. Use

name: Electric Sheep

name: Club for Growth Action

name: University of Notre Dame
notes: Private Catholic research university in Indiana. Major AI initiatives: AI@ND hub, Lucy Family Institute for Data & Society, Scientific AI Initiative. name: Cornell University
notes: Major research university with broad AI activities spanning Cornell Tech (NYC), the AI Initiative, and the MacArthur-funded AI, Policy, and Practice (AIPP) initiative focused on AI's societal impacts. name: Neuralink
notes: Brain-computer interface company co-founded by Elon Musk (2016). Developing implantable BCI (N1 chip) for paralysis patients. name: Federation of American Scientists
notes: DC-based science and technology policy nonprofit founded in 1945. FAS runs the Day One Project to translate scientific expertise into federal policy; active in AI governance, advocating for responsible AI innovation, NIST AISI funding, and federal AI adoption. Submitted AI Action Plan recommendation

name: UK government's Frontier AI Taskforce
notes: UK government body established April 2023 with £100m mandate to evaluate frontier AI safety risks ahead of the Bletchley Park AI Safety Summit. name: International Telecommunication Union
notes: UN specialized agency for information and communication technologies. Hosts annual AI for Good Global Summit. name: UNESCO
notes: UN agency that adopted the first global standard on AI ethics -- the Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence (2021), applicable to all 194 member states. Developed after 3 years of consultation with experts from 155 countries. Framework centers on human rights, transparency, fairness

name: Department of Energy
notes: Federal agency overseeing U.S. energy systems and nuclear infrastructure. name: AI4ALL
notes: Nonprofit co-founded in 2017 by Fei-Fei Li, Olga Russakovsky, and Rick Sommer to increase diversity in AI. Grew from 2015 Stanford summer program for high school girls. 2022 strategic pivot to College Pathways — accelerating underrepresented college students into AI internships and research. Partner

name: Stanford Vision and Learning Lab
notes: Stanford Vision and Learning Lab (SVL), co-directed by Fei-Fei Li, focuses on computer vision, embodied AI, and robotics. Key projects include BEHAVIOR benchmark for household AI agents and the PAIR group for generalizable robot perception. Li has called for science-based AI governance.

name: Darden School of Business
notes: University of Virginia's graduate business school. Home to the LaCross Institute for Ethical Artificial Intelligence in Business, launched Sept 2024 with a $62M gift from the LaCross family (one of the largest AI gifts to any business school globally, from a broader $101M commitment). name: University of Pennsylvania
notes: Ivy League research university with major AI research infrastructure. The ASSET Center (Penn Engineering) focuses on AI safety, explainability, and trustworthiness, with NSF funding. name: Berkeley AI Safety Initiative for Students (BASIS)
notes: Student-run AI safety organization at UC Berkeley. Runs a DeCal course on AI safety, a research program, AI Policy Reading Group, and tech education workshops. Affiliated with SPAR (Supervised Program for Alignment Research) and the broader EA-adjacent safety community.

name: Cambridge AI Safety Hub
notes: AI safety community hub at the University of Cambridge, co-directed by Gaurav Yadav and Justin Dollman. Runs MARS (Mentorship for Alignment Research Students), an AI Safety Fast-Track program, a writing programme, and regular paper reading groups with external researchers.

name: NYU School of Medicine
notes: NYU Grossman School of Medicine at NYU Langone Health, a leading academic medical center. name: SecureBio
notes: Nonprofit biosecurity organization founded in 2022 by MIT professor Kevin Esvelt. Develops AI biosecurity benchmarks and evaluations cited by all major frontier labs (Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta, OpenAI, xAI). Funded partly by effective altruism philanthropy.

name: Collège de France
notes: Prestigious French academic institution (est. 1530) in Paris. name: LinkedIn

name: Technische Universität Berlin

name: Dartmouth College
notes: Private Ivy League research university in Hanover, NH. name: Trust::Data Alliance

name: Small Business Administration

name: City University of New York
